commands.review_summary:
The PR routes BytePlus video submit and poll success JSON reads through the shared bounded provider JSON reader and updates provider tests with streamed JSON fixtures and overflow cases.

PR surface: Source -2, Tests +160. Total +158 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: current main calls `await response.json()` for both BytePlus submit and poll success bodies, and the shared replacement reader has a clear 16 MiB cap and cancellation path. I did not run a live repro in this read-only review.

target.title: fix(byteplus): bound video-generation success response reads
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

`readBytePlusJsonResponse` in `extensions/byteplus/video-generation-provider.ts` reads both the **submit task** and **poll status** success JSON with an unbounded `await response.json()`. The BytePlus endpoint is untrusted external content; a hostile or misconfigured `baseUrl` can stream an arbitrarily large body on either path with no byte ceiling, buffering the whole payload into memory — a memory-pressure / hang (OOM) vector. The sibling video **buffer download** in the same file was already bounded with `readResponseWithLimit`; this closes the remaining unbounded JSON reads.

## Evidence

Reuses the shared bounded reader `readProviderJsonResponse` (16 MiB cap, cancels the stream on overflow) — same pattern as the recently merged #95218 / #96027 / #96038. Plugin-scoped, no new abstraction; the provider-owned `... malformed JSON response` error is preserved.

Real behavior proof (live vitest, not mocked): two real `ReadableStream` overflow tests (submit and poll), each asserting `JSON response exceeds 16777216 bytes` is thrown, the stream is canceled, and the body is not fully pulled.

```
Tests  13 passed (13)
```
target.author: alix-007
